key_insight: The paper surveys finite-strain constitutive models for magneto-active polymers, showing how increasingly thermodynamically consistent frameworks capture anisotropy, nonlinearity, and dissipation under magnetic fields.
engineering_relevance: These models support the design and simulation of smart soft materials for sensors, actuators, vibration control, adaptive structures, and soft robotics.
method: Reviews semi-empirical to nonlinear continuum formulations at finite strains
Covers isotropic, anisotropic, invariant-based, spectral, variational, polyconvex, microstructural, and magneto-viscoelastic models
Compares the governing variables, energy functions, coupling mechanisms, and assumptions behind each approach
evidence_signal: The abstract reports a structured survey of prior constitutive frameworks rather than new experiments or benchmark data, emphasizing model classes and their reported capabilities.
watch_item: Practical use still depends on reliable parameter identification and robust numerical implementation, especially for coupled thermo-magneto-mechanical and microstructure-sensitive effects.
paper_title: Constitutive modelling of magneto-active polymers at finite strains: A survey
